channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Tongogara Monument set for commissioning
item.description: THE late General Josiah Magama Tongogara Monument and Health Centre in neighboring Mozambique is now ready for commissioning, with preparations having reached an advanced stage. The Josiah Magama Tongogara Memorial Site in Mozambique is located in Magacaia Village, Massinga Distr #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: IN PICTURES -Stakeholders urged to leverage Zimbabwe’s global tourism accolades
item.description: Nqobile Bhebhe TOURISM stakeholders have been urged to take pride in the growing list of international accolades that Zimbabwe continues to earn, saying the honours have elevated the country’s profile on the global tourism stage and should be aggressively leveraged to market the destination. [caption id=attachment_5396221 align=alignleft width=680] Stakeholders urged to leverage Zimbabwe’s global tourism accolades[/caption] Permanent Secretary in the Ministry of Tourism and Hospitality Industry, Dr Takaruza Munyanyiwa, made the call during an oversubscribed consultation meeting on the National Tourism Sector Strategy (NTSS) 2026-2030 in Bulawayo on Tuesday. They are yours, be proud of the accolades. Sometimes I feel that as stakeholders we are in the terraces. This is our thing, we must run with it in your marketing and strategies, said Dr Munyanyiwa. Let’s see that hunger to ride on these accolades. Let us harness our collective wisdom, resources, and energy to build our sector. Together, we will shape a vibrant, resilient, and globally competitive tourism sector that uplifts communities, strengthens our economy and reaffirms Zimbabwe’s place on the world tourism map. His remarks come as Zimbabwe continues to receive prestigious international recognition, with the accolades placing the country firmly on the global tourism map and enhancing its visibility among international travellers and investors. Over the past year, Zimbabwe won the Gold Award in the Internet and Media Category at the prestigious 2026 Chinese Tourist Welcome (CTW) Awards, recognising the country’s innovative destination marketing initiatives targeting the Chinese market. Zimbabwe also strengthened its international standing after being recognised by Forbes Magazine as the Best Country to Visit in 2025, a development hailed as a resounding affirmation of the nation’s tourism revival and growing international confidence in Brand Zimbabwe. On the other hand, Tourism and Hospitality Industry Minister Barbara Rwodzi was named Africa’s Tourism Minister of the Year at ITB Berlin 2026, reflecting growing global confidence in the country’s tourism sector. The growing international recognition has significantly boosted Zimbabwe’s destination brand, reinforcing its competitiveness in the global tourism marketplace and providing stakeholders with a strong platform to attract more visitors, investment and tourism business. The National Tourism Sector Strategy (2026-2030) is expected to provide a roadmap for accelerating tourism growth, enhancing competitiveness and increasing the sector’s contribution to economic development. The consultations brought together Government, the private sector, interest groups and other tourism stakeholders to develop a roadmap that will guide the industry’s growth over the next five years, anchored on improved air connectivity, regional integration, destination competitiveness and sustainable tourism development. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

Opera #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

Continue reading "Tshabangu Praises Mnangagwa Government Despite Economic Meltdown" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: NECs called on to build strong foundation by embracing innovation
item.description: Judith Phiri THE Government has called on National Employment Councils (NECs) to build a strong foundation by embracing innovation, strengthening institutional capacity and positioning themselves as strategic partners in national development. Zimbabwe has 48 NECs which are statutory, bipartite bodies that regulate minimum wages, working conditions and dispute resolution within specific industries. In a keynote address and officially opening the 1th Annual Symposium for Chairpersons, Councillors and General Secretaries, Public Service, Labour and Social Welfare Minister Edgar Moyo said as the labour market continues to evolve, NECs must build on a strong foundation by advancing. “Embrace innovation, strengthen institutional capacity and position yourselves as strategic partners in national development. Beyond their traditional responsibilities, NECs should support enterprise competitiveness, promote productivity, facilitate skills development and generate labour market information that contributes to evidence-based policy formulation,” he said. “I am encouraged by the progress already being made by many NECs. The increasing use of digital technologies and social media has improved stakeholder engagement, communication and public awareness of NEC services.” He said these initiatives demonstrate a commitment to ensuring that labour institutions remain accessible and responsive in an increasingly digital environment. The Minister said equally commendable was the expansion of some NECs’ operations through the establishment of provincial and district offices, bringing labour administration services closer to employers and workers throughout the country. “This decentralisation improves access to justice, strengthens compliance and enhances service delivery. It is also pleasing to note that many NECs have also invested in staff development, stakeholder engagement, public awareness programmes and strategic partnerships,” he added. “These initiatives continue to strengthen social dialogue, improve compliance with labour standards and contribute to increased productivity, investor confidence and sustainable economic growth.” He said while they celebrate these achievements, they must also acknowledge that the world of work is undergoing unprecedented transformation. Minister Moyo said the continued expansion of the informal economy, rapid technological advancement, automation, artificial intelligence, digitalisation, climate change and global economic uncertainties are reshaping employment patterns and the skills required for future success. “These developments require labour market institutions that are agile, forward-looking and capable of anticipating change rather than merely responding to it. “NECs must therefore continue to promote innovation, lifelong learning and practical solutions that prepare employers and workers for the future while strengthening Zimbabwe’s labour market resilience,” he said. The two day event convened through the partnership between the NECs and the Ministry of Public Service, Labour and Social Welfare is running under the theme, “Enhancing NECs’ Resilience and Adaptability in a Dynamic Labour Market Towards Vision 2030.” The symposium provides an important platform to reflect on the future of labour administration, exchange experiences, strengthen partnerships and identify practical solutions that will enable NECs to remain effective, relevant and responsive in a dynamic labour environment. In attendance this year are also a delegation from the Namibia Labour Inspection Exchange Programme. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Succession battle reaches boiling point as Generals resist Mnangagwa’s extended term and Constitution Amendment 3 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Harare – A profound political crisis is brewing at the heart of Zimbabwe’s ruling Zanu PF party, as a formidable group of retired military commanders openly challenges President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s bid for an extended term and the controversial Constitution Amendment Act No. 3. This unprecedented resistance, spearheaded by former Air Vice-Marshal Henry Muchena, signals a [...] The post Succession battle reaches boiling point as Generals resist Mnangagwa’s extended term and Constitution Amendment 3 first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Air Zimbabwe’s London route a game-changer for tourism, says PS
item.description: Nqobile Bhebhe PERMANENT Secretary in the Ministry of Tourism and Hospitality Industry, Dr Takaruza Munyanyiwa, has urged tourism industry players to aggressively market Air Zimbabwe as part of the country’s destination offering, describing the resumption of direct flights between Harare and London after a 14-year hiatus as a landmark achievement that will transform Zimbabwe’s tourism prospects. Speaking during consultations on the National Tourism Sector Strategy (NTSS) 2026-2030, Dr Munyanyiwa said the strategy was being crafted at a pivotal moment, marked by significant policy and infrastructure developments that would strengthen Zimbabwe’s competitiveness as a tourism destination. Air Zimbabwe is expected to deploy a leased 302-seater Airbus A330 aircraft on the route. Dr Munyanyiwa said the restoration of the Harare-London service was a major milestone that deserved national recognition. I want to say the NTSS is happening at a very crucial time with our national airline, Air Zimbabwe resuming London flight today and this is 14 years later. This is a major milestone and we need to applaud Government for getting us here. NTSS is a defining moment in our collective journey to shape the future of Zimbabwe’s tourism sector. This is why we are here, said Dr Munyanyiwa. He noted that having a functioning national airline placed Zimbabwe among a select group of African countries with such strategic assets. Just to put into context, out of the 54 countries in Africa there are 14 national airlines so we are among the best. Dr Munyanyiwa said the direct London service would fundamentally change how Zimbabwe positions itself in international tourism markets, as travellers increasingly prefer destinations that offer seamless air connectivity. What it means now is as you package your products, our story from today is different because we have had tourists coming here via different routes, tourists want a direct flight. You cannot be a principal destination if you do not have direct connectivity. Our national airline is flying that route (London), it means we can provide connectivity to the region and also what we need now is a robust domestic connectivity arrangement, he said. He challenged tourism operators to incorporate Air Zimbabwe into their travel packages and marketing campaigns to maximise the benefits of the restored route. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: OneMoney Transforms Rural Healthcare Through Mudhumeni WeHutano Initiative @263Chat @NetOneCellular
item.description: Kakora Village, Chiweshe – NetOne’s mobile financial services platform, OneMoney, in partnership with ZimSmart Villages, has strengthened rural healthcare delivery through the donation of bicycles, blood pressure machines and glucometers to the Mudhumeni WeHutano initiative at Maria Theresa Hospital in Kakora Village, Chiweshe. The equipment [...] The post OneMoney Transforms Rural Healthcare Through Mudhumeni WeHutano Initiative appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: ZRP launches anti-crime campaign in Bulawayo @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Gibson Mhaka THE Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) on Thursday will roll out a national anti-crime campaign in Bulawayo aimed at strengthening the fight against drug and substance abuse, stock theft, sexual offences and other criminal activities through enhanced community participation. The campaign, to be held at the Large City Hall, is expected to bring together members of the public, community leaders, business representatives, youths and other stakeholders as police intensify efforts to promote safer communities. Held under the theme A Safer Nation Starts With Us, the campaign seeks to reinforce the message that crime prevention is a shared responsibility requiring the active participation of every citizen. The initiative comes as the police continue to encourage members of the public to work closely with law enforcement agencies by reporting criminal activities and suspicious behaviour in their communities. The campaign will focus on raising awareness about the dangers of drug and substance abuse, which has emerged as one of the country’s most pressing social challenges, particularly among young people. Police will also engage communities on measures to curb stock theft, a crime that continues to threaten the livelihoods of many rural households, while educating the public on the prevention and reporting of sexual offences and other crimes. The event is expected to emphasise the importance of timely reporting of crime, with the police urging members of the public to embrace the See Something, Say Something approach as part of efforts to build a crime-free society. According to the event programme, the campaign will run under the rallying call: Working Together for a Crime-Free Society – Together We Can, Together We Will. The ZRP says community policing remains one of the most effective tools in combating crime, as it fosters trust and cooperation between law enforcement officers and the public. Members of the public have been encouraged to attend the campaign and interact with police officers on issues affecting the safety and security of their communities. The campaign forms part of the ZRP’s nationwide drive to enhance public awareness, strengthen partnerships with communities and promote collective responsibility in preventing crime across the country. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Bulawayo family appeals for help to find missing elderly woman
item.description: Raymond Jaravaza, Zimpapers Reporter A BULAWAYO family is appealing to members of the public for assistance in locating a missing woman who disappeared almost two and a half years ago in February 2024. 65-year-old Nomthandazo Ndubiwa (65) disappeared from her home in Bulawayo after an argument over business dealings with her estranged husband, according to missing granny sister Mrs Nokuthula Zulu. My sister has been missing for a few years after she and her estranged husband disagreed over the handling of finances from a motor spares business that they were operating together, The couples’ daughter who was financing her parents’ joint business venture passed away in the UK in November 2022. Thats when the misunderstanding between the couple started until it led to be disappearance of the missing granny, according to Mrs Zulu. We have been trying to locate my sister to no avail since she left Zimbabwe to buy motor spares in Botswana with her then husband, said Mrs Zulu The two had been running a business together, which had been financed by their daughter as a way of supporting her parents so they had no choice but to work together even when their relationship and marriage was not in a good space, said Mrs Zulu. Police reports about the missing granny were made to authorities in Zimbabwe and Botswana about more than two years later a breakthrough into what happened to Ndubiwa remains elusive. The family seeks assistance to locate their loved one. We are traumatised as a family. My sister is an elderly woman who is missed by her family. We appeal to the public in both Zimbabwe and Botswana, where she was supposedly last believed to be headed to assist us, she said. Mrs Zulu said the husband of the missing granny is believed to be in the neighbouring country and has reportedly professed ignorance about the whereabouts of Ndubiwa. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Danish aquaculture technology heads to Zimbabwe
item.description: Theseus Mauruki Shambare ZIMBABWE’S aquaculture sector is set for a major technological upgrade following Government’s backing of the importation of modern Danish fish farming equipment, a move expected to accelerate fish production and support the country’s drive to attain fish self-sufficiency and build a US$1 billion fisheries industry. The Ministry of Agriculture, Mechanisation and Water Resources Development has endorsed Fin Fish Suppliers’ application for an import duty and Value Added Tax (VAT) waiver on a full cage fish farming system valued at US$37 701.40 to be imported from Denmark. In a letter to the Ministry of Finance, Economic Development and Investment Promotion seen by Zimpapers reporters, Agriculture Secretary Professor Obert Jiri said the investment aligns with Government’s strategy to expand aquaculture and strengthen food and nutrition security. He said fish farming had become a strategic pillar for Zimbabwe’s economic revival. As you may be aware, fish farming is a strategic pillar for Zimbabwe’s economic revival, food and nutrition security, said Prof Jiri. By diversifying agricultural exports, curbing malnutrition and creating rural employment, the sector directly aligns with national growth targets. In the letter, Prof Jiri said Zimbabwe’s aquaculture industry was still developing and required targeted support to unlock its full potential. He said exempting the imported equipment from duty and VAT would lower the high capital costs associated with commercial fish farming, making the industry more accessible while stimulating investment. The equipment is a long-term investment that comes with ripple effects including job creation, increased fish production and support for national food and nutrition security, he said. The ministry said the project, to be implemented by Fin Fish Suppliers in Lake Kariba South, Binga District, has the capacity to produce up to 7 200 tonnes of fish annually. It said the investment supports the Agriculture and Food Systems Transformation Strategy, the Zimbabwe Tilapia Value Chain Market Strategy and the transboundary Lake Kariba Blue Economy Strategy. Responding to questions from Zimpapers, Fin Fish Suppliers director Mr Tinashe Chihota confirmed the authenticity of the ministry’s letter and said the company had already taken delivery of the Danish cage system, which will soon be floated in Binga. We can confirm that the letter is authentic and we have already received the cage system. The next stage is to float it in Binga as we move into commercial production, he said. Mr Chihota said he appreciated Government’s continued support for young entrepreneurs venturing into aquaculture, saying the enabling policy environment was giving youth-owned businesses confidence to invest in large-scale fish production. We sincerely appreciate how Government is supporting and empowering youth fish farmers. Such interventions lower investment costs and encourage young Zimbabweans to participate in the blue economy, he said. He said besides investing in cage fish production, Fin Fish Suppliers recently received another consignment of premium European Union-certified Aller Aqua fish feed from Denmark to ensure farmers have access to high-quality feed. Mr Chihota said the feed delivers an average feed conversion ratio of below 1.4, allowing farmers to reduce production costs by about 30 percent while achieving faster fish growth. Looking ahead, he said the company was eager to see the establishment of the planned cold-chain facility under Government and development partner support, saying it would strengthen fish marketing and reduce post-harvest losses. We are looking forward to the establishment of the cold-chain facility as Government and its development partners work towards increasing national fish production to 60 000 tonnes and achieving self-sufficiency, he said. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News
